timeline.label: Q1 2026: revenue $2,262M (+3% reported); diluted EPS $1.42 (+6%); U.S. companion animal revenue down 11% operationally while international companion rose 15%
FY2025 results: revenue $9,467M (+2%); net income $2,673M; diluted EPS $6.02; issued $2.0B convertible notes and $1.85B senior notes while repurchasing $3.2B of stock
2026 guidance: operational revenue growth 3-5%; adjusted net income growth 3-6%
Completed $600M debt tender; long-term debt rose to $9.0B year-end 2025
Issued $2.0B convertible senior notes due 2032 to fund capital return program
Raised quarterly dividend 15% to $0.50 per share (annualized $2.00)
Divested medicated feed additive (MFA) business; livestock revenue ex-MFA becomes primary segment lens
Investor Day 2023: outlined innovation pipeline including Librela expansion and parasiticide portfolio
FY2022: revenue $8,080M; launched Librela (bedinvetmab) for canine osteoarthritis pain in additional markets
FY2021: revenue $7,776M; companion animal recovery post-COVID clinic visits
Kristin Peck succeeded Juan Ramón Alaix as CEO (spin-era leadership transition complete)
IPO spin-off from Pfizer; Zoetis begins trading as independent animal health leader

industry.structure: Global animal health is an oligopoly led by Zoetis, Merck Animal Health, Elanco, and Boehringer Ingelheim, with high R&D barriers, veterinarian channel relationships, and durable branded franchises in companion parasiticides and livestock vaccines.
industry.share_shift: Zoetis is gaining share in companion parasiticides (Simparica Trio #1 U.S. canine parasiticide, >$1B U.S. sales) while U.S. companion dermatology faced 2025-2026 softness; international companion and livestock ex-MFA are growing faster than the U.S. companion base.
industry.trend: Pet humanization drives chronic-care and parasiticide spend; livestock demand normalizes post-MFA divestiture; generics and private-label pressure exist but innovation (Librela, long-acting biologics) and direct-to-vet data tools defend pricing.
